topic: The Life of the Holy Prophet Muhammad S.A.W.

subUnits.title: 7.1 The Prophet's Life Before Revelation
notes: • Born in Makkah in the Year of the Elephant (570 CE)
• Father: Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib (died before his birth)
• Mother: Aminah bint Wahb (died when he was 6)
• Grandfather: Abd al-Muttalib (raised him until age 8)
• Uncle: Abu Talib (raised him after grandfather's death)
• As was Arab custom, sent to foster mother Halimah al-Sa'diyah
• Spent early childhood with Banu Sa'd tribe in the desert
• This gave him pure Arabic language and strong physique
• Known for excellent character even before Prophethood
• Never worshipped idols, never participated in pagan rituals
• Avoided the vices of pre-Islamic society: gambling, alcohol, adultery
• Known as 'Hanif' - follower of pure monotheism of Ibrahim
• Worked as a shepherd in youth
• Later became a merchant, known for honesty in trade
• Employed by Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, a wealthy businesswoman
• Impressed by his honesty and character, she proposed marriage
• Married Khadijah at age 25; she was 40 years old
• Happy marriage lasting 25 years until her death
• Children: Qasim, Abdullah, Zainab, Ruqayyah, Umm Kulthum, Fatimah
• As he approached 40, Muhammad (SAW) sought solitude for reflection
• Would retreat to Cave of Hira on Jabal al-Nur (Mountain of Light)
• Spent days in worship, meditation, and contemplation
• Disillusioned with the idolatry and injustice of Makkah
• Sought truth and guidance from the Creator
• For six months, he saw true dreams that came true like daylight
• This prepared him for receiving revelation
• Increased desire for contemplation and worship
• Never participated in idol worship or pagan festivals
• Even enemies acknowledged his trustworthiness and truthfulness
- Orphanhood: Developed compassion for the weak
- Illiteracy: Ensured Qur'an could not be accused of being his composition
- Excellent character: Made him a credible messenger
- Business experience: Taught him leadership and management
- Marriage to Khadijah: Provided emotional and financial support
Did He not find you an orphan and give refuge? And He found you lost and guided you? And He found you poor and made you self-sufficient?
Did We not expand for you your breast? And We removed from you your burden which weighed upon your back? And We raised high for you your repute
• Excellence in character prepared him for divine mission
• Allah prepares those He chooses for great tasks
• Trust in Allah's wisdom and timing
• Time alone with Allah brings guidance
• Muslims should make time for contemplation
• He rejected idolatry before receiving revelation
• Muslims should reject falsehood even before knowing the full truth
• Orphanhood and loss taught him patience
• Hardships prepare us for greater responsibilities
• The Prophet's life before revelation was a preparation for his mission
• His excellent character made him credible when he received revelation
• Allah's preparation shows divine wisdom in choosing messengers
• Muslims should emulate his character and trust in Allah's plan

subUnits.youtubeQuery: Prophet Muhammad life before revelation SSS1 IRS site:youtube.com

quiz.q: The Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was born in:
quiz.explanation: He was born in Makkah in the Year of the Elephant (approximately 570 CE).

quiz.q: The Prophet's father died:
quiz.explanation: Abdullah died before Muhammad (SAW) was born, so he never knew his father.

quiz.q: Who raised the Prophet after his mother's death?
quiz.explanation: After Aminah died when he was 6, his grandfather Abd al-Muttalib raised him until age 8, then uncle Abu Talib.

quiz.q: The title 'Al-Amin' given to the Prophet means:
quiz.explanation: Al-Amin means 'The Trustworthy' - a title given by Makkans even before his Prophethood.

quiz.q: The Prophet worked as a merchant for:
quiz.explanation: He worked for Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, who later proposed marriage due to his honesty.

quiz.q: The Prophet would retreat for contemplation in:
quiz.explanation: He would retreat to Cave of Hira on Jabal al-Nur for worship and reflection.

quiz.q: For how many months did the Prophet experience true dreams before revelation?
quiz.explanation: For six months, he saw true dreams that came true like daylight, preparing him for revelation.

quiz.q: The verse 'Did We not expand for you your breast?' is in Surah:
quiz.explanation: This verse is in Surah Al-Sharh (94:1), referring to Allah preparing the Prophet's heart for revelation.

quiz.q: A lesson from the Prophet's pre-prophetic life is:
quiz.explanation: Allah prepared the Prophet through experiences that developed his character for the prophetic mission.

quiz.q: The Prophet rejected idolatry:
quiz.explanation: He never worshipped idols or participated in pagan rituals, even before receiving revelation.

subUnits.title: 7.2 The Prophet's Mission After Revelation
notes: The First Revelation and Immediate Response:
• First revelation came in Ramadan, 610 CE, at age 40
• Prophet returned home trembling, covered by Khadijah
• Khadijah comforted him and took him to Waraqah ibn Nawfal
• Waraqah confirmed this was the same revelation given to earlier prophets
• Prophet began inviting people to Islam secretly
- Ali ibn Abi Talib (his cousin) - first male child believer
- Abu Bakr al-Siddiq - first adult male believer
• Met in Dar al-Arqam (house of Arqam) for teaching
• Focus on core beliefs: Tawhid, Prophethood, Hereafter
• Commanded to invite people openly: Surah Al-Hijr (15:94)
فَاصْدَعْ بِمَا تُؤْمَرُ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
So declare what you are commanded and turn away from the polytheists
• Climbed Mount Safa and called Quraysh tribes
• Warned them of punishment if they rejected the message
• Abu Lahab (uncle) openly opposed and was condemned in Qur'an
• Mocked the idea of resurrection and judgment
• Bilal ibn Rabah tortured under hot sun
• Rocks thrown at him in Ta'if
• Plot to assassinate him in Makkah
• Muslims found refuge with Christian king (Negus)
• Umar's conversion was a major victory
• Death of Khadijah and Abu Talib
• Loss of emotional and tribal protection
• Sought support but was rejected and stoned
5. Night Journey and Ascension (Isra and Mi'raj):
• Spiritual journey from Makkah to Jerusalem to heavens
• First Pledge (621 CE): 12 men pledged to worship Allah alone
• Second Pledge (622 CE): 73 men and 2 women pledged protection
• This paved way for Hijrah to Madinah
• Left Makkah with Abu Bakr, hid in Cave of Thawr
• Arrived in Quba, then entered Madinah
• Established brotherhood between Muhajirun and Ansar
• Laws revealed for worship, transactions, family
O you who covers himself, Arise and warn, And your Lord glorify, And your clothing purify, And uncleanliness avoid
الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي
This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you
• Muslims should be patient in difficulties
• Started with close circle, then public
• Forgave enemies at conquest of Makkah
• Religion was perfected during his lifetime
• No need for new prophets or revelations
• The Prophet's mission transformed Arabia and the world
• His life is the perfect example for Muslims
• Following his Sunnah is the path to success
• His mission continues through his teachings and example
diagramDescriptions: Timeline of prophetic mission: Secret invitation → Public invitation → Persecution → Hijrah → Madinah → Conquest
Map showing migration routes: Makkah to Abyssinia, Makkah to Madinah
Diagram of key events: Cave of Hira, Dar al-Arqam, Cave of Thawr, Masjid al-Nabawi
Chart showing phases of mission: Makkah (13 years) vs Madinah (10 years)
Illustration of Farewell Sermon at Arafat
subUnits.youtubeQuery: Prophet Muhammad mission after revelation SSS1 IRS site:youtube.com

quiz.q: The first revelation came to the Prophet in the month of:
quiz.explanation: The first revelation came in Ramadan, as confirmed in Surah Al-Baqarah (2:185).

quiz.q: The first person to accept Islam was:
quiz.explanation: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid was the first person to believe in the Prophet's message.

quiz.q: The secret invitation phase lasted approximately:
quiz.explanation: The Prophet invited people secretly for about 3 years before public proclamation.

quiz.q: The command to invite people openly is in Surah:
quiz.explanation: Surah Al-Hijr (15:94) commands the Prophet to declare openly what he was commanded.

quiz.q: The migration to Abyssinia was primarily to:
quiz.explanation: Muslims migrated to Abyssinia to escape persecution from Quraysh, finding refuge with the Christian king.

quiz.q: The Year of Sorrow refers to the death of:
quiz.explanation: In 619 CE, both Khadijah (his wife) and Abu Talib (his protector) died, causing great sorrow.

quiz.q: The Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj) resulted in the prescription of:
quiz.explanation: During Mi'raj, the five daily prayers were prescribed as a gift to the Muslim Ummah.

quiz.q: The Hijrah to Madinah marks the beginning of:
quiz.explanation: The Hijrah (622 CE) is the starting point of the Islamic lunar calendar.

quiz.q: The conquest of Makkah was characterized by:
quiz.explanation: The Prophet entered Makkah peacefully in 630 CE and granted general amnesty to its people.

quiz.q: The verse 'This day I have perfected for you your religion' indicates:
opts: Religion was completed during the Prophet's life
quiz.explanation: Surah Al-Ma'idah (5:3) confirms that Islam was completed during the Prophet's lifetime.
